data.title: ITM Power GAAP EPS of 2.30p, revenue of £18.03M; reaffirms full-year guidance
data.description:
• ITM Power Plc press release (ITMPF): 1H GAAP EPS of 2.30p.
  • Revenue of £18.03M (+16.1% Y/Y).
  • Adjusted EBITDA loss of £11.9m (H1 2025: £16.8m).

  • Cash on 31 October 2025 of £197.8m (31 October 2024: £203.1m).

  • Contract backlog to date of £152m, up from £43.7m two years ago, and now consisting of 71% profitable contracts as we work through our legacy contracts, up from 60% in April 2025.

  •   Reiteration of full-year guidance:

    o  Revenue between £35m and £40m

    o  Adjusted EBITDA loss between £27m and £29m

    o  Cash £170m-£175m

data.title: Alvotech resolves global patent disputes with Regeneron and Bayer over Eylea biosimilar
data.description:
Alvotech (ALVO) has reached a licensing and settlement agreement with Regeneron (REGN) and Bayer (BAYZF), resolving all remaining patent disputes worldwide related to Alvotech’s biosimilar to Eylea 2 mg, which is approved for marketing in the European Economic Area, United Kingdom and Japan.

In combination with the previously announced agreement, which granted a Q4 2026 license entry date for the U.S., pending regulatory approval, Alvotech now has worldwide rights to manufacture and supply its global commercial partners with the aflibercept biosimilar, it said in a statement.

The settlement allows Alvotech (ALVO) and partners to market the biosimilar from January 1, 2026, in the UK and Canada; from May 1, 2026, in the EEA and other countries (excluding the U.S.) and Japan (excluding DME); and from November 1, 2026, in Japan with all approved indications.

The remaining terms of the agreement remain confidential.

data.title: Waymo says NHTSA opens probe into Santa Monica pedestrian incident
data.description:
The National Highway Traffic Safety Administration ((NHTSA)) plans to open an investigation into a Jan. 23 incident where one of Waymo’s vehicles made contact with a young pedestrian.

Waymo said the event occurred in Santa Monica when the “pedestrian suddenly entered the roadway from behind a tall SUV, moving directly into our vehicle’s path.”

“Our technology immediately detected the individual as soon as they began to emerge from behind the stopped vehicle. The Waymo Driver braked hard, reducing speed from approximately 17 mph to under 6 mph before contact was made.”

Following the event, Waymo voluntarily contacted the NHTSA on the day of the incident and said it would cooperate fully with the investigation.

Waymo (WAYMO), the autonomous driving unit of Alphabet (GOOGL) (GOOG), is reportedly in talks to raise more than $15B at a valuation approaching $100B in a financing round led by its parent company.

Waymo's services are currently operational in the San Francisco Bay Area, Los Angeles, Phoenix, Austin, Miami, and Atlanta. In 2026, the firm plans to begin autonomous ride-hailing in 20 additional cities, including Tokyo and London.

data.title: Alibaba joins hands with China's Zelos for $2B robovan business - report
data.description:
• Alibaba's (BABA) logistics arm, Cainiao, is merging its autonomous-driving unit with China's Zelos Technology to form a new robovan business valued at around $2B, The Wall Street Journal reported, citing people familiar with the matter.
  • Alibaba will gain an equity stake in Zelos Technology, while Cainiao Robovan will continue to operate as an individual brand after the merger.
  • The merged entity will be run by Zelos, and a Cainiao executive will join Zelos’s board, the people said. 
  • A formal deal announcement is expected by Thursday, the report added.
